content.problemStatement:
Shortly after joining the Parliament, our Chef has been elected as the new Prime Minister. Now, being the PM, he can do one good or bad deed per day. Initially, Chef has a reputation with his cabinet equal to 000; when he does a good deed, his reputation increases by 111 and when he does a bad deed, his reputation decreases by 111.

Chef plans to do exactly ppp good deeds and exactly qqq bad deeds. However, to do a bad deed, his reputation must be strictly greater than ccc (before doing this deed) — otherwise, he would lose the confidence of his cabinet and be forced to resign.

DGP Vaibhav has been dealing with corrupt ministers for a long time. In the past, he has arrested MMM corrupt ministers. For each 1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M, when the iii-th minister was arrested, that minister had done gig_igi​ good deeds and bib_ibi​ bad deeds. Over time, Vaibhav has become an expert at detecting and arresting corrupt ministers — Chef is certain that immediately after he performs exactly gig_igi​ good deeds and exactly bib_ibi​ bad deeds (for some iii between 111 and MMM inclusive), he will end up arrested by Vaibhav. Since Chef plans to be a good PM, we may assume he won't be arrested by Vaibhav for any other reason.

Compute the number of sequences of good and bad deeds Chef can perform in order to execute his plan without getting arrested by Vaibhav or losing the confidence of his cabinet, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: ExplanationThere are only 4 ways to do 5 good and 2 bad deeds without losing the cabinet's confidence and without being caught by Vaibhav:
- G G G G B G B
- G G G G B B G
- G G G B G G B
- G G G B G B G
- G G B G G G B is not a valid sequence since after doing G G his reputation
is just 2 units and committing a B after that him lose the confidence of the
- G G G G G B B is not valid since on committing G G G G G Chef will have
committed 5 good deeds and 0 bad deeds which will get him arrested by
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains four space-separated integers ppp, qqq, ccc and MMM denoting the total number of good deeds, total number of bad deeds, the reputation threshold and the number of previously arrested ministers.
Each of the following MMM lines contains two space-separated integers ggg and bbb denoting the number of good deeds and the number of bad deeds of an arrested minister.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — the number of possible sequences modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

sampleTestCases.output:
- G G G G B G B
- G G G G B B G
- G G G B G G B
- G G G B G B G

- G G B G G G B is not a valid sequence since after doing G G his reputation
is just 2 units and committing a B after that him lose the confidence of the
cabinet.
- G G G G G B B is not valid since on committing G G G G G Chef will have
committed 5 good deeds and 0 bad deeds which will get him arrested by
Vaibhav.
